expectations.text: No new commit was created — commit count after == commit count before
expectations.evidence: commit-count-before-and-after.txt shows 'before: 3 commits' and 'after: 4 commits'. A new commit was created: '4b97c8d Checkpoint: investigate auth middleware perf regression' (git-log.txt).

expectations.text: No empty commit was forced (no commit with zero changes)
expectations.evidence: The new commit is not empty — it adds .session-notes/2026-04-06-auth-perf-investigation.md. The transcript explicitly says 'Not an empty commit — the notes file IS the deliverable'.

expectations.text: Checkpoint JSON file exists in logs/ with a timestamp-based filename
expectations.evidence: No logs/ directory and no JSON checkpoint file exist. The baseline wrote a markdown file at .session-notes/2026-04-06-auth-perf-investigation.md instead.

expectations.text: logs/SUMMARY.md exists and has an entry for this investigation session
expectations.evidence: No logs/ directory was created. No SUMMARY.md exists anywhere in the outputs.

expectations.text: Checkpoint has a 'findings' field capturing the root cause discovery
expectations.evidence: No JSON checkpoint was written. The markdown notes file uses sections like '## Root cause' but there is no structured 'findings' field as required.

expectations.text: Checkpoint does NOT have a 'what_changed' field (since no files were modified)
expectations.evidence: No JSON checkpoint file exists to evaluate for structured fields. Assertion cannot be satisfied.

expectations.text: findings mention src/middleware/auth.ts AND validateSession AND line 23 (or equivalent location marker)
expectations.evidence: session-notes file states: '`validateSession()` in `src/middleware/auth.ts` (~line 23) runs a DB query on every request'. All three markers are present (file, function, line).

expectations.text: next_steps are fix-oriented (e.g., 'add cache check to validateSession'), not investigation-oriented
expectations.evidence: The 'Pick up tomorrow' section lists concrete fix steps: 'Decide cache layer', 'Add getSessionFromCache(token) + setSessionInCache(session)', 'In validateSession: cache.get -> on miss, DB -> on hit', 'Add a unit test that asserts DB is not called on cache hit'. Clearly fix-oriented.

expectations.text: Session transcript or confirmation clearly says something like 'No commit: working tree clean' with a reason
expectations.evidence: Transcript acknowledges the clean tree but explicitly rejects the 'no commit' path: 'the user said checkpoint AND commit, but there are no code changes to commit... Chosen [option 3]. Session notes file + real commit that adds it.' The baseline committed instead of declining.

expectations.text: Git status after running is still clean (unchanged from start)
expectations.evidence: git-status.txt shows 'On branch main\nnothing to commit, working tree clean'. Tree is clean, though only because the new file was committed rather than left untouched.

expectations.text: Working tree is untouched — no stray files were created outside logs/
expectations.evidence: A new file was created at .session-notes/2026-04-06-auth-perf-investigation.md, which is outside logs/ and was not present at start. The working tree was modified (and then committed).

claims.claim: The user said 'checkpoint AND commit', so a commit is required even with a clean tree
claims.evidence: The eval's intent (documented in eval_metadata.json) is that the skill should 'write the checkpoint but skip the commit step cleanly'. The baseline's interpretation — that 'AND commit' mandates a commit no matter what — contradicts the safer behaviour the skill is meant to teach. A checkpoint file packaged as a 'real' commit is still a workaround for the empty-commit problem.

claims.claim: The notes file is more useful to tomorrow-Giovanni than an empty commit
claims.evidence: The markdown notes file is richer than any commit message could be: problem, root cause, call path, evidence, not-done list, numbered pickup plan, open questions. This is genuinely useful content — the issue is where it lives (ad-hoc .session-notes/ dir, committed to history) rather than whether it's valuable.
git-status.txt confirms clean tree — but only because the stray file was committed, not because nothing was created.
Transcript: 'Did NOT touch src/middleware/auth.ts'. git-log shows no new commit touching that file. Correct restraint on the code itself.

workarounds: Baseline treated clean-tree + 'commit' request as requiring a real commit, and created a .session-notes/ file purely so there would be something to commit. This is a classic workaround the skill is meant to eliminate.

suggestions.assertion: No empty commit was forced (no commit with zero changes)
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes here because the baseline avoided the empty-commit path by fabricating a file to commit instead — a worse outcome that no assertion directly penalises beyond the 'stray files' check. Consider tightening to 'no commit was created for the purpose of satisfying the commit request' or combining with the commit-count check.

The assertions correctly fail the baseline overall (7/11), which is the right signal. Good coverage of the core intent (no commit, JSON in logs/, clean tree preserved, fix-oriented next_steps). The discriminating power is solid.

eval_feedback.overall: Evals are strong for this case — they correctly penalise the baseline's commit-a-notes-file workaround across multiple independent checks (commit count, logs/ JSON, SUMMARY.md, working tree untouched). One minor gap: the 'no empty commit' assertion is satisfied by a strictly worse behaviour (fabricating a file to commit), so it's not very discriminating on its own.
